text:
*[Bug 18015] tools:add_datalist silently ignores Rdata files, only accepts RData files. Proposal to allow both, or warn, or at least document*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18015>

Sebastian Meyer (<mailto:seb.meyer@fau.de|seb.meyer@fau.de>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
           Severity|normal                      |enhancement

--- Comment #5 from Sebastian Meyer (<mailto:seb.meyer@fau.de|seb.meyer@fau.de>) ---
Thanks for taking a look. I still think the documentation of the
`tools::add_datalist()` build helper should *not* duplicate the list of
supported data formats; `?data` is the right place for this information -- with
all the relevant details.

That said, the `\seealso` section is now (r90199) more explicit about the
reference:

    -  \code{\link{data}}.
    +  \code{\link{data}}, for a description of the supported data formats.

More importantly, the documentation needed clarification that 'datalist' files
are irrelevant for packages using 'LazyData'. (@CRAN: >200 packages using
'LazyData' do supply a 'datalist' file, but that is unused and sometimes
outdated; R CMD check should probably suggest removal.)

(In reply to Simon Dedman from comment #3)
> the way it operates invisibly could be done more helpfully

Yes, the current return value (`NULL`) is indeed unhelpful and also not
documented. I think that `add_datalist()` should at least report if it actually
did write `data/datalist`.  It could be made a logical value and then also be
used by R CMD build to report the result of:

    * looking to see if a 'data/datalist' file should be added
